ucs.title: [LLM] Rancor (Unit42) Office-doc → msiexec.exe remote-MSI fetch (DDKONG/PLAINTEE installer chain)
ucs.description: Hunts Rancor's signature delivery chain: a politically-themed Office document spawns msiexec.exe with an HTTP/HTTPS URL argument to pull a remote MSI installer (DDKONG / PLAINTEE / KHRAT loaders). This abuses the trusted, signed msiexec binary as a proxy executor (T1218.007) immediately after macro-driven user execution (T1204.002) and is the most consistent observable across Unit42-tracked Rancor campaigns against SE Asian government/diplomatic targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as image values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe") Processes.process_name="msiexec.exe" (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.dest Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename firstTime as firstTime_p lastTime as lastTime_p | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest) as remote_dest from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="msiexec.exe" OR All_Traffic.process_name="msiexec.exe" by host] | convert ctime(firstTime_p) ctime(lastTime_p) | sort - count
ucs.kql:
// Rancor — Office app spawning msiexec.exe to fetch a remote MSI (DDKONG/PLAINTEE delivery chain)
let Lookback = 14d;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe"]);
let RemoteMsi =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"                                  // exclude machine accounts
    | where FileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)               // chain anchor: Office/script host parent
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("http://","https://")          // remote-fetch flag — Rancor signature
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/(i|q|quiet|qn|package)\b" // /i, /q, /qn install switches
    | extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              MsiCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              RemoteUrl;
// Confirm msiexec actually went out to the internet (kills test/dev FPs)
RemoteMsi
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "msiexec.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl_Net = RemoteUrl
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (Timestamp .. Timestamp + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, Parent, ParentCmd, MsiCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl_Net, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Rancor — Office app spawning msiexec.exe to fetch a remote MSI
let Lookback = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688                                                 // process create
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\msiexec.exe"
| where ParentProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\\(winword|excel|powerpnt|outlook|wscript|cscript|mshta)\.exe$"
| where CommandLine has_any ("http://","https://")
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\s/(i|q|quiet|qn|package)\b"
| extend RemoteUrl = extract(@"(?i)(https?://[^\s\"']+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, RemoteUrl
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Rancor Office-Doc Msiexec Remote MSI Fetch
id: a4c1b2e6-9f1a-4f67-8c1d-rancor-msiexec-01
status: experimental
description: Detects msiexec.exe spawned by Office or script host with an HTTP(S) URL argument — Rancor's DDKONG/PLAINTEE installer chain (Unit42, June 2018)
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0075/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/unit42-rancor-targeted-attacks-south-east-asia-using-plaintee-ddkong-malware-families/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.007
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0075
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\msiexec.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  selection_cmdline:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
  selection_install_flag:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\s/(i|q|quiet|qn|package)\b'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate enterprise software deployment via Office-launched setup links (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Rancor (Unit42 G0075) is one of the few SE-Asia-focused crews that consistently chains macro-driven Office docs to msiexec.exe pulling a remote MSI as the staging step for DDKONG and PLAINTEE. The combo of (1) Office/script-host parent + (2) msiexec child + (3) HTTP(S) URL in cmdline + (4) install switches is essentially a Rancor fingerprint — generic ransomware/loader crews overwhelmingly prefer

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `RemoteUrl` is not a column of SecurityEvent; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Rancor WMI event-consumer persistence following Office macro execution (PLAINTEE/DDKONG persistence)
ucs.description: Hunts Rancor's persistence step: shortly after a politically-themed Office document executes, a WMI permanent event subscription (__EventFilter / CommandLineEventConsumer / FilterToConsumerBinding) is created — the persistence mechanism observed for PLAINTEE and DDKONG implants. Pairs T1546.003 with the actor's known T1566.001→T1204.002 entry to lift it above generic WMI-persistence noise.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("wmic.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","schtasks.exe") (Processes.process="*__EventFilter*" OR Processes.process="*CommandLineEventConsumer*" OR Processes.process="*FilterToConsumerBinding*" OR Processes.process="*ActiveScriptEventConsumer*" OR (Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*"))) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true earliest(_time) as office_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") by host Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where firstTime - office_time < 600 AND firstTime - office_time > 0 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) ctime(office_time) | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// Rancor — WMI event-subscription / scheduled-task persistence within 10 min of Office macro execution
let Lookback = 14d;
let PersistWindow = 10m;
let OfficeProc =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project OfficeTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, OfficeParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, OfficeChild = FileName;
let PersistAct =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("__EventFilter","CommandLineEventConsumer","ActiveScriptEventConsumer","FilterToConsumerBinding","/NAMESPACE:\\\\root\\subscription"))
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("__EventFilter","CommandLineEventConsumer","Set-WmiInstance","Register-WmiEvent","root\\subscription"))
         or (FileName =~ "schtasks.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/create")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              PersistTool = FileName, PersistCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              PersistParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
PersistAct
| join kind=inner OfficeProc on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (OfficeTime .. OfficeTime + PersistWindow)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, OfficeTime)
| project OfficeTime, PersistTime = Timestamp, DelaySec, DeviceName, AccountName,
          OfficeParent, OfficeChild, PersistTool, PersistCmd, PersistParent
| order by PersistTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Rancor — WMI/scheduled-task persistence within 10 min of Office macro execution
let Lookback = 14d;
let PersistWindow = 10m;
let OfficeProc =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\\(winword|excel|powerpnt)\.exe$"
    | project OfficeTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, OfficeParent = ParentProcessName, OfficeChild = NewProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("__EventFilter","CommandLineEventConsumer","ActiveScriptEventConsumer","FilterToConsumerBinding","root\\subscription"))
     or (NewProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\\p(ower)?sh(ell)?(\.exe)?$" and CommandLine has_any ("Set-WmiInstance","Register-WmiEvent","__EventFilter","root\\subscription"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\schtasks.exe" and CommandLine has "/create")
| project PersistTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, PersistTool = NewProcessName, PersistCmd = CommandLine, PersistParent = ParentProcessName
| join kind=inner OfficeProc on Computer
| where PersistTime between (OfficeTime .. OfficeTime + PersistWindow)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', PersistTime, OfficeTime)
| project OfficeTime, PersistTime, DelaySec, Computer, Account, OfficeParent, OfficeChild, PersistTool, PersistCmd, PersistParent
| order by PersistTime desc
ucs.rationale: Rancor's PLAINTEE / DDKONG implants are documented (Unit42, 2018) using WMI permanent event subscriptions and scheduled tasks for persistence — both rare on user endpoints. Generic WMI-subscription detections drown in legitimate management agents (SCCM, Tanium); requiring an Office-app-spawned ancestor within 10 minutes anchors the hit to the actor's macro-borne entry vector and turns a noisy hunt
